skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: Data set X consists of the heights of 60 trees and has a mean of 20 meters. Data set Y consists of the heights of 40 trees and has a mean of 55 meters. Data set Z consists of the heights of the 100 trees from data sets X and Y. What is the mean, in meters, of data set Z?
rationale: Total height in X: 60 × 20 = 1,200. Total height in Y: 40 × 55 = 2,200. Combined total: 1,200 + 2,200 = 3,400 for 100 trees. Mean of Z: 3,400 / 100 = 34.
